about.title: About
about.lede: Twenty-seven years in civilian civil engineering. Academic, consulting, and patent record traceable to publications, registration numbers, and stamped reports.
bio: Dr. Milad Khatib is a Beirut-based civil engineering consultant, academic, and patent-holder. He holds a PhD in Structural and Geotechnical Engineering from Beirut Arab University (2018), is currently completing an MBA at the American University of Science and Technology, and holds assistant-professor and research-supervisor roles at the Lebanese International University, the University of Balamand, and ISSAE-Cnam Liban. His published record includes fifty-two peer-reviewed contributions across IWA, Springer, Elsevier, and Taylor & Francis, twenty-one editorial and reviewer positions at international journals, and a book published on Amazon. He has been an OEA Beirut member since 1999.
memberships: Editorial / reviewer roles at 21 international journals — see /editorial

services.title: Services
services.lede: Three pillars. Each grounded in published research and stamped on signed reports.

structural.title: Structural Engineering
structural.lede: Post-tensioned and prestressed concrete; seismic mitigation; fiber-reinforced systems; monostrand anchorage; inverted-U-shaped reinforcement.
methodology: ACI 318 and Eurocode 2 verification on every design review
Inverted-U-shaped reinforcement methodology (Khatib, Case Studies in Construction Materials, 2020)
Finite-element re-analysis using ETABS, ABAQUS, ANSYS
representative: Inverted-U shear-strength enhancement — book and 2020 paper
Monostrand anchorage in unbonded post-tension flat-slab systems (Heliyon 2024)
AI-assisted seismic isolator optimization (forthcoming, EC8 / UBC-97)
when: A serviceability or shear-capacity finding needs an independent peer-grounded re-analysis.
A design verification must withstand a sworn deposition.
An academic collaboration requires an industry co-author with stamped credentials.

geotechnical.title: Geotechnical Engineering
geotechnical.lede: Slope stability; dam grouting by GIN method; diaphragm walls; groundwater dam comparative analysis; Lebanese-terrain water-resource engineering.
methodology: GIN-method curtain grouting parameter envelopes (Khatib, Water Practice & Technology, 2022)
Thermo-mechanical analysis of insulated diaphragm walls in PLAXIS 2D (2025)
Gravity-vs-arch dam comparative load and seepage modelling
Site-specific characterisation of Lebanese clay and limestone substrates
representative: Curtain grouting recommendation envelope, Lebanese gravity dam (2022)
Insulated diaphragm-wall PLAXIS case study, urban excavation (2025)
Groundwater dam — comparative analysis between gravity and arch (2025)
Slope stability and water-quality risk assessment, Alkalaa, South Lebanon (2024)
when: A dam or curtain-grouting envelope needs defensible re-validation.
An urban excavation requires a thermo-mechanical PLAXIS case-study before signoff.
A water-resource project needs published methodology behind the recommendation.

forensic.title: Forensic & Expert-Witness
forensic.lede: Defensible technical opinions for insurers, law firms, and courts. Methodology grounded in published research; conclusions earned through evidence.
methodology: Methodology before conclusion — every report opens with the analytical framework
Published-research backing on every methodology citation (DOIs in every report)
Bilingual reports (English / Arabic) on request, RTL-correct typesetting
Sworn-deposition-ready: limitations and assumptions stated explicitly
representative: Inverted-U reinforcement re-analysis methodology (CSCM 2020) — applied to flat-slab failure
Monostrand anchorage re-validation (Heliyon 2024) — applied to post-tension serviceability
GIN-method dam grouting envelopes (WPT 2022) — applied to seepage failure analysis
when: An insurance carrier needs an independent technical opinion before settlement.
A law firm requires a sworn expert with publication-grounded methodology.
A court requires a bilingual report with both English and Arabic registers.
forensic.jurisdictions: Lebanese Republic · Gulf Cooperation Council on retainer
forensic.retainer: Standard retainer model with capped re-analysis hours; specific commercial terms quoted per matter.

foodCollector.title: Food-Particle Collection Device for Post-Eating Hygiene

contact.title: Contact
contact.lede: Direct contact below. Inquiries are answered within two business days, in English or Arabic.

form.name: Name
form.success: Thank you. Dr. Khatib will reply within two business days.
form.error: Could not send. Please email milad@miladkhatib.com directly.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: This site collects no analytics cookies. Contact-form submissions are routed to Dr. Khatib's email and retained for the duration of the inquiry. No data is shared with third parties beyond the email-delivery provider (Resend).

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.body: Content on this site is informational. Engineering opinions are issued only after a signed engagement letter. Lebanese law governs any matter arising from use of this site.

imprint.title: Imprint
imprint.body: Dr. Milad Khatib · Civil Engineering Consultancy · Beirut, Lebanese Republic. OEA Beirut member since January 1999. Civilian engineering practice with concurrent academic posts at the Lebanese International University, the University of Balamand, and ISSAE-Cnam Liban.
